title: A Year After Government Cuts: Workers Ask What Was Saved
summary: One year ago, President Trump made big cuts to the government to save money. This affected many workers, like Thea Price, who worked at the United States Institute of Peace. She and 300 others were fired, then rehired, then fired again, which made their lives very hard. The cuts were led by a group called DOGE, which wanted to stop waste in the government. DOGE says it saved $215 billion by cutting jobs and other things, but over 260,000 workers lost their jobs. Now, people like Thea are asking if the savings were worth all the trouble and pain they went through. The article shows that while some money was saved, the human cost was high, leaving workers confused and hurt.

background_read: This article talks about government cuts that happened one year ago under President Trump. It focuses on workers like Thea Price who lost their jobs at the United States Institute of Peace. A group called DOGE led these cuts to save money by reducing waste. While DOGE claims to have saved billions, many workers suffered, and people are questioning if it was worth it. This helps us understand how government decisions can affect real people's lives.
Article_Structure: WHO: Thea Price and other workers at the United States Institute of Peace, along with over 260,000 federal workers.
WHAT: Government cuts led by DOGE that caused job losses and confusion.
WHERE: In Washington and across the United States government.
WHY: To save money and reduce waste, but it hurt many workers.

perspectives.description: Workers like Thea Price feel hurt and confused by the job cuts, wondering if the savings were worth their pain.
